017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.tracing;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
020
021/**
022 * SPI for receiving per-request tracing events from {@code juneau-rest-server}.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * {@link #startSpan(RestRequest)} fires immediately before every {@code @RestOp} handler runs &mdash;
026 * the returned {@link Scope} is closed by the framework after the handler completes (including the
027 * exception path). Bridge implementations create whatever the downstream tracer expects (an
028 * OpenTelemetry {@code io.opentelemetry.api.trace.Span}, a Brave {@code Span}, an application
029 * tracing object, etc.) and surface the close / status / error transitions through the {@code Scope}.
030 *
031 * <h5 class='topic'>Off-by-default contract</h5>
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * {@code juneau-rest-server} resolves the {@code TracerHook} via
035 * {@code RestContext.getBeanStore().getBean(TracerHook.class)}. When no bean is supplied,
036 * {@link NoOpTracerHook#INSTANCE} is used &mdash; the framework never reaches for a tracer, never
037 * inspects headers for W3C trace context, and adds no per-request cost beyond a single static-field
038 * read plus a single {@code AutoCloseable.close()} on a singleton noop scope. To opt in, the consumer
039 * registers a {@code TracerHook} bean &mdash; typically via {@code @Bean TracerHook} on the resource
040 * or its parent.
041 *
042 * <h5 class='topic'>Usage</h5>
043 *
044 * <p class='bjava'>
045 *    <ja>@Rest</ja>
046 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> RestServlet {
047 *
048 *       <ja>@Bean</ja>
049 *       <jk>public</jk> TracerHook tracer(OpenTelemetry <jv>otel</jv>) {
050 *          <jk>return new</jk> OtelTracerHook(<jv>otel</jv>);
051 *       }
052 *    }
053 * </p>
054 *
055 * <h5 class='topic'>Lifecycle</h5>
056 *
057 * <p>
058 * For each {@code @RestOp} invocation, the framework:
059 * <ol>
060 *    <li>Calls {@link #startSpan(RestRequest)} just before parameter resolution, passing the in-flight
061 *       {@link RestRequest} so the bridge can read trace-context headers
062 *       ({@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate}) and set request-derived attributes.
063 *    <li>Runs the handler (which may throw).
064 *    <li>Calls {@link Scope#setStatusCode(int)} with the resolved response status, then
065 *       {@link Scope#setError(Throwable)} if the handler threw, then {@link Scope#close()} &mdash;
066 *       always in a {@code finally} block so the span closes even on exception.
067 * </ol>
068 *
069 * <p>
070 * Implementations <b>must</b> be thread-safe &mdash; the same {@code TracerHook} bean is invoked
071 * concurrently from every request thread for the lifetime of the {@code RestContext}.
072 *
073 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
074 *    <li class='jc'>{@link NoOpTracerHook}
075 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Scope}
076 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.metrics.MetricsRecorder}
077 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerObservability">REST Server &mdash; Observability (Micrometer + OpenTelemetry)</a>
078 * </ul>
079 *
080 * @since 10.0.0
081 */
082@FunctionalInterface
083public interface TracerHook {
084
085   /**
086    * Opens a new span for the in-flight request.
087    *
088    * @param request The in-flight {@link RestRequest}. Never <jk>null</jk>. Bridges may read headers
089    *    for distributed-tracing context propagation (e.g. W3C {@code traceparent} / {@code tracestate}),
090    *    the HTTP method, the path, etc. Bridges <b>must not</b> mutate the request.
091    * @return The opened {@link Scope}. Never <jk>null</jk>. Returning {@code null} is a contract
092    *    violation &mdash; bridges that cannot open a span (e.g. because the trace context is invalid)
093    *    should return {@link NoOpTracerHook.NoOpScope#INSTANCE} so the framework's close-in-finally
094    *    contract still holds.
095    */
096   Scope startSpan(RestRequest request);
097
098   /**
099    * Opens a new span for a custom (non-request) observation.
100    *
101    * <p>
102    * Unlike {@link #startSpan(RestRequest)}, this entry point is <b>not</b> tied to an in-flight
103    * {@link RestRequest} &mdash; it is the substrate for the explicit programmatic observation API
104    * ({@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.observation.Observations}) so application code can trace an
105    * arbitrary block of work that has no associated HTTP request. Bridge implementations typically open
106    * a span of kind {@code INTERNAL} (rather than {@code SERVER}) named {@code spanName}.
107    *
108    * <p>
109    * The default implementation returns {@link NoOpTracerHook.NoOpScope#INSTANCE} &mdash; a bridge that
110    * does not override this method simply does not trace custom observations, and the no-backend path
111    * stays zero-allocation. Both shipped bridges (the OpenTelemetry {@code TracerHook}) override it.
112    *
113    * @param spanName The span name (e.g. {@code "loadOrder"}). Never <jk>null</jk>; never blank.
114    * @return The opened {@link Scope}. Never <jk>null</jk> &mdash; implementations that cannot open a
115    *    span must return {@link NoOpTracerHook.NoOpScope#INSTANCE} so the close-in-finally contract holds.
116    */
117   default Scope startSpan(String spanName) {
118      return NoOpTracerHook.NoOpScope.INSTANCE;
119   }
120}
